channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Family of 9 Dies After Taking Dangerous Shortcut in Nashik
item.description: In a tragic incident in Nashik, a family of nine lost their lives when their car plunged into a well. The family, seeking a quicker route, decided to avoid the main road and take a shortcut. Unfortuna...

item.title: Aaron Ramsey Announces Retirement from Professional Football
item.description: Wales captain and former Arsenal midfielder Aaron Ramsey has officially retired from professional football, marking the end of a distinguished career spanning over a decade and a half. The 35-year-old...

item.title: Emakhazeni Municipality Struggles with Billing, Infrastructure, and Water Services
item.description: The Emakhazeni local municipality in Mpumalanga has been facing serious administrative and infrastructure challenges. In the 2024/25 financial year, the municipality recorded a R21-million deficit, th...

item.title: National Bureau of Investigation Investigates Shooting Incident in Palawan
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) is now looking into the details of a recent shooting incident that took place in Palawan. While specific details about the incident remain limited, authoriti...

item.title: Alberta's Separatist Movement Faces Legal Hurdles Ahead of Referendum
item.description: Alberta's separatist movement is gaining momentum as the province inches closer to a referendum on its future within Canada. Led by the Alberta Prosperity Project, the push for independence is a respo...

item.title: The Struggle for African Consciousness in South Africa’s Foreign Policy
item.description: The article critically examines South Africa's engagement with the BRICS bloc and its failure to prioritise African revolutionary thought and Black Consciousness in its political approach. It highligh...

item.title: Malabon parish faces criticism for tying dove to balloons in Salubong ceremony
item.description: Animal welfare organizations have raised concerns over a recent practice at a Malabon parish where a dove was tied to balloons during the traditional 'Salubong' event. The Philippine Animal Welfare So...

item.title: Australian War Memorial to Update Ben Roberts-Smith Display Following Arrest
item.description: The Australian War Memorial has announced it will update the plaque on its Ben Roberts-Smith display following the former special forces soldier's arrest. Despite allegations of misconduct in Afghanis...

item.title: Aikansia Muro’s Journey in Promoting Customer-Focused Banking in Tanzania
item.description: Aikansia Muro ni kiongozi muhimu katika sekta ya benki hapa Tanzania, sasa akiwa Kichwani mwa Huduma za Wateja wa NMB Bank Plc. Kwa zaidi ya miaka 16 kwenye sekta ya kifedha, Aikansia amejenga taaluma...

item.title: Posters targeting Akhilesh Yadav in UP ahead of 2027 elections draw comparison to Karachi’s Lyari
item.description: Ahead of the 2027 Uttar Pradesh Assembly elections, controversial posters inspired by the Bollywood film *Dhurandhar* have appeared in parts of Lucknow and Amethi, targeting Samajwadi Party chief Akhi...

item.title: Governor Mbah Commends DSS for Arresting Kidnappers and Gunrunners in Enugu
item.description: Governor Peter Mbah of Enugu State has praised the Department of State Services (DSS) for successfully arresting notorious kidnappers and gunrunners in the state. Speaking at the DSS headquarters in E...

item.title: Resident Doctors Resume Work After NARD Suspends Nationwide Strike
item.description: The Nigerian Association of Resident Doctors (NARD) don suspend their nationwide strike, wey mean say resident doctors for University College Hospital (UCH), Ibadan go resume work by 8:00 a.m. on Wedn...

item.title: Albert Ojwang’s Family Honors His Memory with Traditional Ceremony for Widow Nevnina Omondi
item.description: Nine months after the tragic death of activist and teacher Albert Ojwang, his widow, Nevnina Omondi, has warmed the hearts of Kenyans with her graceful transformation and renewed spirit. A recent vide...

item.title: Nine teens charged after stolen police car recovered following Townsville incident
item.description: Nine teenagers have been charged after a stolen police vehicle incident in Townsville, north Queensland, left three officers injured. Police were responding to reports of several stolen cars being dri...

item.title: Smile Hair Clinic introduces structured hairline symmetry benchmarks for treatment planning
item.description: Smile Hair Clinic in Istanbul has formalised benchmarks for hairline symmetry within its treatment-planning workflow, reflecting a broader trend in Turkey's health tourism sector. With Turkey hosting ...

item.title: Analysis of US-Iran Rescue Operation Involving 155 Aircraft at Isfahan
item.description: The article was meant to discuss the complex operation involving the deployment of 155 aircraft to the Isfahan site, which remains enveloped in multiple uncertainties. It appears to focus on the US-Ir...

item.title: Tark Group Directors Held Personally Liable for Covid PPE Contract Irregularities
item.description: The Special Tribunal has ruled that Tark Group directors, Katleho O'Hara Mokonyane and Bonelela Mgudlwa, are personally liable for R14-million in illegal profits from two contracts awarded during the ...

item.title: How the Antarctic Circumpolar Current Shaped Earth's Climate
item.description: A powerful ocean current, the Antarctic Circumpolar Current (ACC), is one of the strongest natural forces on Earth, carrying more water than all of the world’s rivers combined. Recent research reveals...

item.title: Coastal Women Seek More Decision-Making Power in Tanzania’s Blue Economy
item.description: Women along Tanzania’s coastline play a central role in the country’s blue economy, from farming seaweed to restoring marine ecosystems. Despite this, they are often excluded from key decisions shapin...

item.title: Skydiver in his 50s dies in Kent after parachute failure
item.description: A skydiver in his 50s has tragically died at Headcorn Aerodrome in Kent after his parachute reportedly failed to open during a jump. Emergency services rushed to the scene after witnesses saw the skyd...
